Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she consumes 0.5 ounces over a two-day period. In a leap year with 366 days, she will wash her hair 183 times, resulting in a total usage of 45.75 ounces for the entire year. Since each bottle contains 14 ounces, she will need to divide the total amount by the bottle size, which equals approximately 3.27, meaning she must purchase 4 bottles to ensure she has enough supply until [date].

To guarantee she does not run out before the year ends, she should buy 4 bottles at [store_name] at the beginning of the year. This calculation accounts for the extra day in a leap year and ensures that the total volume of 56 ounces from four bottles exceeds her required 45.75 ounces. By planning ahead and purchasing the correct number of containers, she can maintain her hair care routine without interruption throughout the 366-day period.
